title: Apply for a vehicle investigation
description: Ask the Barbados Licensing Authority to investigate a vehicle that is off the road.
summary: If a vehicle is off the road, you can ask the Barbados Licensing Authority to investigate it. The investigation confirms key details like the registration number, chassis number, engine number, make, model and condition.
People usually ask for an investigation when they need to clear up the record on a vehicle – for example, to settle an insurance claim, to support a sale, or to write the vehicle off.
The Licensing Authority will go to where the vehicle is being kept. You will need to give them the name and phone number of a contact at that location so they can arrange access.
The fee is currently $50 BBD. Pay it at the Barbados Revenue Authority before you apply, and bring the receipt with you.

items: your full name, address, parish and phone numbers
the registration number, make, model, engine number and chassis number
where the vehicle is being kept
the name and phone numbers of the person to contact at that location

items.name: Proof of ownership
items.note: Registration card or bill of sale.

items.name: Receipt of payment for the investigation fee

notes: Pay at the Barbados Revenue Authority before you apply.

how_long: An investigation is usually completed within 10 working days.

options.heading: In person
content: Take the completed Application for Vehicle Investigation form to the Barbados Licensing Authority.

contact.address:
The Pine, East–West Boulevard
St. Michael
BB11000

assumptions: Although the form states a $50 BBD fee, it does not mention how long the investigation usually takes. ‘Within 10 working days’ is an estimate.
Document list (photo ID, proof of ownership) is inferred from standard practice. The form itself does not specify what to bring.
